nativeRange.habitat: Streams and tributaries of lowland rainforest; the Mamberamo River drainage in northern Irian Jaya (West Papua), Indonesia

decorPreferences: Dense planting at sides and back with open central swimming area
Fine-leaved plants or spawning mops for breeding
Floating plants to diffuse light and reduce skittishness
Darker substrate tends to enhance coloration

diet.notes: Generally accepts a wide range of foods including quality flakes, nano pellets, frozen cyclops, brine shrimp, daphnia, and bloodworms. Regular inclusion of live or frozen foods has been reported to intensify coloration and support conditioning for breeding.

goodWith: Amano shrimp and larger freshwater shrimp
avoidWith: Cherry shrimp and small dwarf shrimp (likely to be predated)

imageCandidates.notes: Anandarajkumar; 2418x1539; sharp two-fish group showing the schooling look, iridescent blue-green bodies with red dorsal/anal edges and red tails, both males in full colour.
7TP (Krzysztof Bartosik); 6016x4000; tack-sharp single male, vivid violet-blue diamond-scaled body with bright red fins and tail, highest resolution of the set.
Francisco Eliel; 1024x683; sharp two-fish group against a blue background, silvery-blue iridescence with red fin edges and tails, adds group variety.

summary: The Neon Dwarf Rainbowfish (Melanotaenia praecox) is one of the smallest members of the rainbowfish family, with males developing an iridescent blue body and vivid red-orange fins as they mature. It is a peaceful schooling species well suited to planted community aquariums of 75 litres or more.
careNotes: The Neon Dwarf Rainbowfish is among the smallest members of the rainbowfish family Melanotaeniidae, typically reaching around 5–7 cm in total length in aquaria. Males are considerably more vivid than females — females show a silvery body with yellowish fins. Coloration tends to intensify noticeably with maturity, which can take 1–2 years; young specimens purchased from shops may appear comparatively washed-out. Males display shimmering head stripes during courtship. Best kept in groups of 6 or more; a ratio of roughly 1–2 females per male has been reported to reduce male-to-male squabbling and encourage natural display behavior. An active, open-water swimmer, the species needs ample unobstructed swimming space despite preferring a planted setup. It tends to be somewhat skittish — floating plants to diffuse overhead light and a darker substrate are commonly recommended to help settle the fish. Moderately soft to moderately hard water is preferred; the species is generally considered adaptable to a range of conditions within those parameters, though high water quality with regular partial water changes is advised. As of the 2019 IUCN assessment, the species is listed as Least Concern.
breedingNotes: The Neon Dwarf Rainbowfish is an egg-scatterer that provides no parental care; spawning tends to occur among fine-leaved plants or over a spawning mop. Spawning itself is not considered particularly difficult under appropriate conditions — conditioning adults with live or frozen foods beforehand is commonly recommended. Eggs generally hatch in approximately 7–10 days at typical aquarium temperatures. Fry are very small and have been reported to require microscopic foods (infusoria or commercial fry powder) initially, progressing to baby brine shrimp nauplii after 1–2 weeks. Adult fish will readily eat both eggs and fry, so separating the eggs or using a dedicated breeding setup tends to improve survival rates.

primary.name: Aquarium Co-Op
primary.notes: Primary source for care parameters, diet, compatibility, tank sizing, and breeding overview.

additional.name: SeriouslyFish
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters (temp 23–28°C, pH 6.8–7.5, hardness 5–15°H), tank minimum (55L / 24" footprint), adult size (8 cm max, usually smaller in aquaria), grouping (6–8+), and breeding. Hardness range narrowed to SF's 5–15°H (more precise than AC's general 'harder water' note). pH averaged between SF (6.8–7.5) and user-brief (6.5–8.0) to give 6.5–7.8. Care level noted as 'moderate' by SF — reconciled to intermediate.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name, taxonomy (Melanotaeniidae / Atheriniformes), native range (Mamberamo River, northern Irian Jaya / West Papua, Indonesia), temperature (22–28°C), and maximum standard length (5.0 cm SL). FishBase SL values converted to approximate total length for adultSizeCm. IUCN Least Concern status confirmed.

fish.breedingNotes: The Neon Dwarf Rainbowfish is an egg-scatterer that deposits eggs among fine-leaved plants or spawning mops without providing any parental care. Spawning itself is not considered particularly difficult, but raising fry tends to be more demanding due to their very small initial size and high feeding frequency requirements.
